name: Heterotic string compactification
aliases: E8 x E8 heterotic on Calabi-Yau
summary: Compactify the 10D E8 x E8 heterotic string on a 6D Calabi-Yau manifold with SU(3) holonomy. The condition of unbroken N=1 supersymmetry in 4D forces this geometry; the resulting 4D effective theory has E6 x E8' gauge symmetry and naturally contains SM-like fermion generations. Provides a UV-complete origin for gauge unification, supersymmetry, and (depending on the manifold) flavor structure.

addresses_puzzles.note: All gauge couplings descend from a single 10D string coupling; tree-level unification at the string scale ~ 10^17-18 GeV. Loop and threshold corrections are model-dependent.
Requires SUSY in the 4D effective theory; the hierarchy problem is then handed to the SUSY sector. Embeds in MSSM-like or split-SUSY phenomenology.
Yukawa couplings descend from intersection numbers and Calabi-Yau topology; specific compactifications produce realistic mass hierarchies, but the choice of CY is not unique (landscape problem).

touches_benchmarks.note: String-scale dimension-6 operators produce p -> e+ pi^0 at rates 10^32-10^36 yr depending on compactification; partial overlap with Super-K/Hyper-K reach.

new_symmetries: 10D N=1 SUSY broken to 4D N=1 by Calabi-Yau
introduces.note: Choice of Calabi-Yau (one of ~10^4 known constructions or 10^500 more general flux vacua), string coupling, moduli VEVs. The 'landscape' is what makes this hard to falsify.

history: Candelas, Horowitz, Strominger, Witten (1985), 'Vacuum Configurations for Superstrings', Nucl. Phys. B 258, 46 — the canonical paper that established Calabi-Yau compactification as the route from 10D heterotic strings to 4D N=1 SUSY phenomenology. Subsequent decades developed orbifold, smooth-CY, and free-fermionic constructions; the 'string landscape' problem (10^500 vacua) emerged from systematic surveys of flux compactifications.

references.citation: Candelas, Horowitz, Strominger, Witten, 'Vacuum Configurations for Superstrings', Nucl. Phys. B 258, 46 (1985)
